ksas.statement: Knowledge of data valuation and infonomics methodologies, including frameworks for quantifying data as an enterprise asset and connecting data investment to measurable financial outcomes.

Knowledge of organizational change management principles as applied to large-scale data and AI transformation programs, including adoption barriers, culture change levers, and executive sponsorship requirements.
Knowledge of enterprise transformation program design, including operating model change, capability maturity progression, transformation roadmap construction, and change sequencing principles.
Knowledge of data and AI organizational design patterns, including centralized CoE, federated, and hub-and-spoke models, and the conditions under which each is appropriate.
Knowledge of innovation program design, including ideation methods, prototype development approaches, value hypothesis construction, and stage-gate evaluation criteria.
Knowledge of data and AI maturity assessment frameworks and how to diagnose organizational capability gaps across strategy, governance, architecture, talent, and culture dimensions.
Knowledge of data and AI business case construction, including value driver identification, benefit quantification methods, investment sizing, and risk-adjusted return analysis.

Knowledge of data and AI strategy development approaches, including strategy formulation methods, alignment with enterprise business strategy, competitive positioning through data capabilities, and strategy communication frameworks.

Knowledge of talent management practices for data and AI organizations, including workforce planning methodologies, skills gap analysis, hiring strategy, retention approaches, career path development, and succession planning.

Knowledge of data and AI governance structures at the board and executive committee level, including oversight responsibilities, reporting cadences, decision rights allocation, and escalation frameworks.

Knowledge of the data and AI vendor ecosystem, including major platform providers, emerging technology categories, vendor lock-in risks, open-source alternatives, and strategic partnership evaluation criteria.

Knowledge of data and AI due diligence frameworks for mergers, acquisitions, and private equity transactions, including data asset valuation, technical debt assessment, key person risk evaluation, and integration complexity analysis.
Knowledge of data monetization models and data product strategy, including internal data products, external data licensing, data-enabled services, and data marketplace design principles.
Skill in constructing and defending data and AI investment portfolios using risk-adjusted financial frameworks, including expected value of information, NPV, and ROI analysis.
Skill in building executive alignment for data and AI transformation programs, including stakeholder mapping, coalition building, and resistant stakeholder engagement.

Skill in designing transformation roadmaps that sequence capability development logically, with dependencies identified, quick wins positioned early, and long-term structural changes sequenced appropriately.
Skill in designing and executing applied research projects with clear organizational relevance, balancing research rigor with practical applicability.
Skill in communicating research findings to both technical and non-technical audiences, including paper writing, conference presentation, and internal briefing formats.
Skill in conducting organizational data and AI diagnostic assessments, including stakeholder interviews, artifact review, maturity scoring, and finding synthesis.
Skill in producing executive-level program status reporting, including milestone tracking, risk registers, budget status, and decision requests.
Skill in developing enterprise data and AI strategies that connect organizational business objectives to data capabilities, AI initiatives, governance requirements, and measurable value outcomes.
Skill in building and developing high-performing data and AI teams, including talent acquisition, onboarding design, performance management, career development planning, and succession preparation.
Skill in presenting data and AI strategy, risk, and value to board-level audiences, translating technical complexity into governance-appropriate language and decision frameworks.
Skill in evaluating emerging data and AI technologies for strategic relevance, assessing maturity, organizational fit, competitive impact, and integration requirements before recommending investment.

Skill in managing data and AI investment portfolios across multiple concurrent initiatives, including prioritization frameworks, resource allocation, portfolio performance tracking, and initiative rebalancing.
Skill in negotiating strategic vendor partnerships for data and AI platforms, including commercial terms, roadmap alignment, dependency management, and exit strategy planning.
Report to the board, audit committee, and executive leadership on data and AI risk posture, program maturity, regulatory compliance status, and value realization.
Design and lead enterprise data and AI transformation programs, including operating model redesign, capability development roadmaps, and organizational change management.
Build and lead cross-functional transformation teams, establishing shared objectives, working norms, and accountability structures across organizational boundaries.
Translate research findings into production-applicable methods, working with engineering teams to operationalize novel approaches.
Develop and maintain the enterprise data and AI strategy, including vision articulation, strategic priority identification, capability roadmap construction, and annual strategy refresh.
Conduct data and AI workforce planning, including current-state skills assessment, future-state requirements modeling, gap analysis, and talent acquisition and development strategy formulation.

Manage the data and AI investment portfolio, including initiative prioritization, funding allocation, performance tracking against value hypotheses, and portfolio rebalancing based on realized outcomes.

Conduct data and AI due diligence assessments for M&A and investment transactions, including data asset evaluation, technical architecture review, key person risk assessment, and integration planning.
Establish and operate data and AI governance committees, including charter development, membership selection, decision authority definition, and meeting cadence and agenda management.
Build organizational data culture through executive sponsorship, success story amplification, recognition programs, and visible leadership commitment to data-informed decision-making.
Ability to lead through organizational resistance to data and AI transformation, including navigating political dynamics, competing stakeholder interests, and cultural inertia.
Ability to align diverse stakeholder interests around a shared data and AI vision, building coalitions that sustain transformation momentum through organizational changes and competing priorities.
Ability to make strategic resource allocation decisions under uncertainty, balancing proven approaches with experimental initiatives and adjusting course as evidence emerges.

Ability to identify and develop data and AI leadership talent within the organization, recognizing potential across diverse backgrounds and building leadership pipelines that strengthen organizational capability.
Ability to synthesize market trends, competitive intelligence, technology evolution, and organizational capability into coherent strategic recommendations that inform executive decision-making.
